title: Operating Margin by Industry: Profitability Rankings (2026)
description: Operating and net profit margins across 30+ industries. Rank by profitability, identify the most and least profitable industries, and view 5-year trends.

mainEntity.name: What is a good operating margin by industry?

acceptedAnswer.text: A 'good' operating margin varies drastically by industry. High-margin industries like Software or Pharmaceuticals often see operating margins above 20-30%, while volume-driven industries like Retail or Grocery typically operate on single-digit margins (3-8%).

mainEntity.name: Which industry has the highest profit margins?

acceptedAnswer.text: Historically, industries with high intellectual property and low marginal costs of reproduction—such as Software, Biotechnology, and Capital Markets—tend to have the highest profit margins.

mainEntity.name: What is the average profit margin for a business?

acceptedAnswer.text: Across the broader S&P 500, the average net profit margin typically hovers around 10-12%, though this fluctuates with macroeconomic conditions and inflation.

mainEntity.name: How do you calculate operating margin?

acceptedAnswer.text: Operating margin is calculated by dividing a company's operating income (profit after deducting operating expenses like wages and depreciation, but before interest and taxes) by its total revenue, then multiplying by 100 to get a percentage.

mainEntity.name: Why are gross margins higher than net margins?

acceptedAnswer.text: Gross margin only deducts the direct costs of making a product (COGS). Net margin is lower because it deducts all other expenses, including operating costs, interest, and taxes.
